my_thoughts: 'Should I have said something?'—asking if defense was allowed. Teacher affirms: every right to defend. This removes guilt about potential defense and authorizes future defense. Not 'you should have' (creates guilt) but 'you had right to' (removes guilt, gives permission). Opening door to self-defense as legitimate.
accumulated_insight: T4: Student questions 'Should I have said something?' Teacher empowers: 'You had every right to defend yourself.' Gives permission for past self-defense and future. Predicting ask_deeper_question about how to defend.
visible_response: So is that thinking about a mistake or is it self-torture?
